matches.predicted: Resistance exercise can acutely change testosterone.
matches.gold: Resistance exercise can acutely change testosterone.

matches.predicted: Testosterone is relevant to training adaptations.
matches.gold: Testosterone is relevant to training adaptations.

matches.predicted: 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity cardio, or 75 minutes per week of vigorous-intensity cardio, is tied to cardiovascular benefit.
matches.gold: 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity cardio or 75 minutes per week of vigorous-intensity cardio is the minimum dose tied to cardiovascular benefit.

matches.predicted: GH secretion is tightly linked to sleep, and physiological GH is secreted during slow-wave sleep.
matches.gold: Physiological GH is secreted during slow-wave sleep.

matches.predicted: Sleep, awakenings, and IGF-I modulate the GH secretory response.
matches.gold: Sleep, awakenings, and IGF-I modulate the GH secretory response.

matches.predicted: Fragmented or shortened sleep can alter the normal GH pulse pattern.
matches.gold: Fragmented or shortened sleep can alter the normal GH pulse pattern.

matches.predicted: Sleep deprivation changes exercise-induced GH release.
matches.gold: Sleep deprivation changes exercise-induced GH release.

matches.predicted: Sleep is relevant to testosterone biology, and many “testosterone boosters” lack strong evidence overall.
matches.gold: Many testosterone boosters lack strong evidence overall.

matches.predicted: Sleep restriction is associated with higher injury risk in athletes, especially when reduced sleep coincides with higher training load and fatigue.
matches.gold: Sleep restriction is associated with higher injury risk in athletes, especially when combined with high training load and fatigue.

matches.predicted: A resistance training session acutely increases mTOR signaling in skeletal muscle.
matches.gold: A resistance training session acutely increases mTOR signaling in skeletal muscle.

matches.predicted: The rise in mTOR signaling after a resistance training session is part of the molecular response linked to hypertrophic adaptation.
matches.gold: The post-resistance-training mTOR rise is part of the molecular response linked to hypertrophic adaptation.

matches.predicted: Chronic resistance training alters how strongly mTOR signaling responds to a bout of exercise.
matches.gold: Chronic resistance training and detraining alter how strongly mTOR signaling responds to a bout of exercise.

matches.predicted: Amino acids regulate mTOR in this context.
matches.gold: Amino acids regulate mTOR in this context.

matches.predicted: The post-lift mTOR signaling response is influenced by both the training stimulus and nutritional state.
matches.gold: The post-lift signaling response is influenced by both the training stimulus and nutritional state.

matches.predicted: Beta-alanine is a carnosine substrate used as an ergogenic aid in sport.
matches.gold: Beta-alanine is a carnosine substrate used as an ergogenic aid in sport.

matches.predicted: Beta-alanine is most relevant for repeated high-intensity work or sustained hard efforts where hydrogen ion accumulation becomes a limiter, rather than for every kind of exercise.
matches.gold: Beta-alanine is most relevant for repeated high-intensity work or sustained efforts where hydrogen-ion accumulation is a limiter.

matches.predicted: In winter, gym goers often have lower vitamin D status, especially if they train indoors.
matches.gold: Gym-goers in winter often have lower vitamin D status, especially if they train indoors.

matches.predicted: Low vitamin D is associated with worse muscle performance in athletes.
matches.gold: Low vitamin D is associated with worse muscle performance and higher stress-fracture risk in athletes.

matches.predicted: A very low-volume sprint-cycling protocol did not improve cardiovascular fitness over 6 weeks in physically active young adults.
matches.gold: A low-volume sprint-cycling protocol did not improve cardiovascular fitness over 6 weeks in physically active young adults.

matches.predicted: Sprint interval training improved aerobic performance.
matches.gold: Sprint interval training improved aerobic performance but not maximal cardiac output.

matches.predicted: Tart cherry juice has been studied in a daily dose pattern taken around the hard training window for recovery.
matches.gold: Tart cherry juice studies use a daily dose taken around the hard training window.

matches.predicted: Tart cherry juice is used to aid recovery after strenuous exercise.
matches.gold: Tart cherry juice can aid recovery after strenuous exercise.

matches.predicted: VO2 max is used to assess physical capacity in recreationally active people.
matches.gold: VO2 max is used to assess physical capacity in recreationally active people.

matches.predicted: VO2 max can be estimated in some settings.
matches.gold: VO2 max can be estimated in some settings.

matches.predicted: Eccentric training is at least as effective as other modalities for increasing muscle cross-sectional area.
matches.gold: Eccentric training is at least as effective as other modalities for increasing muscle cross-sectional area.

matches.predicted: Eccentric training has a more consistent edge for strength, power, and stretch-shortening-cycle performance.
matches.gold: Eccentric training has a more consistent advantage for strength, power, and stretch-shortening-cycle performance.

matches.predicted: Strength training is associated with lower all-cause mortality.
matches.gold: Strength training is associated with lower all-cause and cardiovascular mortality.

matches.predicted: Zone 2 training can improve lactate-threshold-related performance mainly by shifting the power or pace you can sustain at a given blood lactate level upward, especially when a lot of training is done below about 2 mM blood lactate.
matches.gold: Zone 2 training shifts the power or pace sustainable at a given blood lactate level upward, especially when most training is below approximately 2 mM blood lactate.

matches.predicted: Excessive time spent near threshold may be counterproductive.
matches.gold: Excessive time spent near threshold may be counterproductive for lactate-threshold adaptation.

matches.predicted: Taurine improved endurance overall.
matches.gold: A meta-analysis found taurine improved endurance overall.

matches.predicted: Dose did not significantly moderate the effect of taurine on endurance.
matches.gold: Dose did not significantly moderate taurine's effect on endurance in the meta-analysis.

matches.predicted: 1 g of taurine taken acutely before or after exercise can decrease lactate levels.
matches.gold: 1 g taurine taken acutely before or after exercise can decrease lactate levels.

matches.predicted: Lifting can lower resting blood pressure.
matches.gold: Lifting can lower resting blood pressure.

matches.predicted: Exercise training in general has been shown to lower systolic and diastolic blood pressure by at least about 10/5 mmHg in many controlled studies.
matches.gold: Exercise training generally lowers systolic and diastolic blood pressure by at least about 10/5 mmHg in controlled studies.

matches.predicted: Sleep loss is associated with lower testosterone.
matches.gold: Sleep loss is associated with lower testosterone.

matches.predicted: Sleep debt can decrease testosterone alongside growth hormone and IGF-1.
matches.gold: Sleep debt can decrease testosterone alongside growth hormone and IGF-1.

matches.predicted: 24 hours of sleep loss impairs weightlifting performance in national-caliber male collegiate weightlifters.
matches.gold: 24 hours of sleep loss impairs weightlifting performance in national-caliber male collegiate weightlifters.

matches.predicted: Endurance training can increase mitochondrial content/density.
matches.gold: Endurance training can increase mitochondrial content/density.

matches.predicted: The field is not settled on whether volume or intensity is the bigger driver of mitochondrial adaptations.
matches.gold: The field is not settled on whether volume or intensity is the bigger driver of mitochondrial adaptations.

matches.predicted: Creatine supplementation is associated with increases in muscle size.
matches.gold: Creatine is associated with increases in muscle size and, in some studies, changes in muscle fiber characteristics.

matches.predicted: For hypertrophy, at least 2 hard sessions per muscle group per week is supported rather than once weekly.
matches.gold: At least 2 hard sessions per muscle group per week is supported over once-weekly training.

matches.predicted: Training volume matters for hypertrophy.
matches.gold: Training volume matters for hypertrophy.

matches.predicted: Multiple-set training generally outperforms single-set training.
matches.gold: Multiple-set training generally outperforms single-set training for hypertrophy.

matches.predicted: Citrulline more reliably raises blood arginine levels than arginine.
matches.gold: Citrulline more reliably raises blood arginine levels than arginine.

matches.predicted: Citrulline has human data showing increased plasma arginine in trained men.
matches.gold: Citrulline has human data showing increased plasma arginine and some performance benefits in trained men.

matches.predicted: Citrulline has a supplementation advantage over arginine because of reduced first-pass metabolism.
matches.gold: Citrulline has a supplementation advantage over arginine due to reduced first-pass metabolism and more efficient conversion to arginine.

matches.predicted: Active recovery generally produces better recovery outcomes than passive recovery after strenuous exercise or between exercise bouts.
matches.gold: Active recovery results in better recovery outcomes than passive recovery after strenuous exercise or between bouts.

matches.predicted: A 48-hour window can be enough for recovery of some biochemical and performance measures after CrossFit-style workouts.
matches.gold: A 48-hour window can be enough for recovery of some biochemical and performance measures after CrossFit-style workouts.

matches.predicted: A 2024 meta-analysis found NAC significantly reduced muscle soreness and post-exercise lactate, but the effect sizes were small.
matches.gold: A 2024 meta-analysis found NAC significantly reduced muscle soreness and post-exercise lactate, with small effect sizes.

matches.predicted: In well-trained cyclists, NAC did not improve repeated intense endurance performance or short-term recovery after a hard effort.
matches.gold: NAC did not improve repeated intense endurance performance or short-term recovery in well-trained cyclists.

matches.predicted: In a muscle-damage model, NAC influenced immune responses after eccentric exercise.
matches.gold: NAC influenced immune responses after eccentric exercise in a muscle-damage model.

matches.predicted: A single workout can acutely increase or decrease blood glucose depending on fasting state, exercise intensity, and timing, while repeated exercise may improve fasting glucose regulation over time.
matches.gold: A single workout can acutely raise or lower blood glucose depending on fasting state, exercise intensity, and timing.

matches.predicted: Fasted exercise can raise glucose from pre- to post-exercise in healthy adults on average.
matches.gold: Fasted exercise can raise glucose from pre- to post-exercise in healthy adults on average.

matches.predicted: Fasted exercise changes insulin and free fatty acids in healthy adults.
matches.gold: Fasted exercise also changes insulin and free fatty acid levels.

matches.predicted: When exercise is done regularly in the fasted state, some longer-term metabolic adaptations may be more favorable in some situations, but not all.
matches.gold: Regular fasted-state exercise may produce more favorable longer-term metabolic adaptations in some situations.

matches.predicted: Therapeutic exercise is supported for rotator cuff problems.
matches.gold: Therapeutic exercise is supported for rotator cuff problems.

matches.predicted: Masters athletes are a distinct group.
matches.gold: Masters athletes are a distinct group.
